steps.title: Use the minimum jar count
steps.content: To maximize large jars while keeping the discount, buy exactly $220$ total jars. Let $l$ be the number of large jars, so the number of small jars is $220-l$.

steps.title: Apply the budget limit
steps.content:
Substitute $220-l$ for the small jars in the cost inequality:
$$5.20(220-l)+12.40l\le 2{,}500$$
$$1{,}144+7.20l\le 2{,}500$$
$$7.20l\le 1{,}356$$
$$l\le 188.33$$

steps.title: Choose the largest whole number
steps.content: The number of large jars must be a whole number, so the maximum possible value is $188$. The answer is 188.
